# rex

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 246
- **Lines of code**: 86,292
- **Languages**: TypeScript (228), JSON (9), Other (4), Markdown (4), Text (1)
- **Zones**: 12 (12 with descriptions)
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-08T23:54:02.887Z

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- God function: buildProposals in src/analyze/propose.ts calls 31 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- Dual cohesion scoring (with and without test files) is a prerequisite for reliable zone-health decisions, not an optional enhancement. At minimum three zones have cohesion scores materially influenced by test co-location (unit-core 0.17, fix 0.25, and the three degenerate zones from finding 13 with test fractions of 71%, 27%, and 50%). All zone dissolution or consolidation decisions should be deferred until this metric is available — acting on contaminated cohesion scores risks misattributing measurement artifacts as structural problems. [warning]
- The god-function finding (cmdPrune, 34 unique function calls, finding 18) and the tight-coupling finding (prune.ts ↔ output.ts, 60 cross-file calls, finding 19) are independently-derived signals pointing to the same source file. This corroboration elevates confidence above single-heuristic noise. The 60-call unidirectional coupling to output.ts is the more actionable signal: it indicates prune.ts drives its own formatting directly rather than delegating through an abstraction, which is the observable behavior associated with a god function that is growing in the CLI presentation direction. [warning]
- Zone taxonomy mixes two incompatible naming conventions: test-tier prefixes (unit-, e2e-, unit-core, unit-cli, unit-store, unit-analyze) and domain-layer names (core, store, cli, fix). This mixing causes zone health metrics to conflate test-suite structure with source code structure in aggregate views. The individual zone findings for fix and unit-core are symptoms of this systemic taxonomy gap. Adopting a consistent taxonomy (e.g. source zones as domain-layer, test zones as domain-tier) would also improve zone detection by giving the algorithm consistent naming signals. [info]

### Relationships

- Tightly coupled modules: src/cli/commands/prune.ts and src/cli/output.ts — 57 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- Isolated files — no import edges between 4 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (.rex)
- High coupling (0.78) — 17 imports target "unit" [warning] (cli)
- Low cohesion (0.22) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (cli)
- 17 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (core)
- High coupling (0.64) — 63 imports target "unit" [warning] (core)
- Low cohesion (0.36) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (core)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (e2e)
- High coupling (0.75) — 4 imports target "unit" [warning] (fix)
- Low cohesion (0.25) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (fix)
- Bidirectional coupling: "core" ↔ "unit" (63+5 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- ... and 17 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "cli" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (cli)
- Zone "Cli" (cli) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.78, cohesion: 0.22, coupling: 0.78) — requires immediate architectural intervention [critical] (cli)
- Zone "core" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (core)
- Zone "Core" (core) has critical risk (score: 0.64, cohesion: 0.36, coupling: 0.64) — requires refactoring before new feature development [warning] (core)
- Zone "fix" was identified from filename patterns, not directory structure — consider creating src/cli/commands/fix/ and moving related files there [info] (fix)
- This zone has 4 files and cohesion 0.25. Before dissolving, confirm whether test files account for ≥50% of its membership (consistent with the measurement-artifact pattern in global finding 13). If cohesion improves substantially when test files are excluded, defer structural action until the dual cohesion metric is available. [info] (fix)
- Zone "Fix" (fix) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.75, cohesion: 0.25, coupling: 0.75) — requires immediate architectural intervention [critical] (fix)
- src/cli/commands/ contains files from 8 zones (cli, core, fix, store, unit, unit-analyze, unit-cli, unit-core) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Hub function: walkTree in src/core/tree.ts is called from 30 files (high fan-in expected for this module type) — changes here have wide impact, consider if responsibilities can be narrowed [info]
- 6 zones exceed architectural risk thresholds (cohesion < 0.4, coupling > 0.6): unit-core, cli, fix, unit, core, store — mandatory refactoring recommended before further development [warning]
- ... and 9 more

| `tsconfig.json` | config |  | root |
| `vitest.config.ts` | config |  | root |

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
